title: Physical Access Outside Approved Maintenance Window at PSP

compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-006-6 R1 Part 1.2 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.13.23: Physical Access Outside Approved Maintenance Window at PSP.

spl:
index=physical sourcetype=pacs:access result="granted" earliest=-7d
| lookup cip_psp_register reader_id OUTPUT psp_name maint_cron_ok
| eval hour=strftime(_time,"%H")
| eval dow=strftime(_time,"%w")
| lookup psp_maint_windows.csv psp_name dow hour OUTPUT in_window
| where in_window!="true"
| table _time, employee_id, psp_name, reader_id, in_window
description: We alert on badge-granted entries at Physical Security Perimeters outside documented maintenance windows for follow-up with operations.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes physical and sourcetypes pacs:access, pacs:access`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Encode maintenance windows in `psp_maint_windows.csv`. (2) Add holiday exceptions. (3) Route alerts to physical security. (4) Correlate with work orders. Indexes required: `index=physical`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pacs:access`, `sourcetype=pacs:access``.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for we alert on badge-granted entries at physical security perimeters outside documented maintenance windows for follow-up with operations. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=physical` `sourcetype=pacs:access`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Encode maintenance windows in `psp_maint_windows.csv`. (2) Add holiday exceptions. (3) Route alerts to physical security. (4) Correlate with work orders.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=physical sourcetype=pacs:access result="granted" earliest=-7d
| lookup cip_psp_register reader_id OUTPUT psp_name maint_cron_ok
| eval hour=strftime(_time,"%H")
| eval dow=strftime(_time,"%w")
| lookup psp_maint_windows.csv psp_name dow hour OUTPUT in_window
| where in_window!="true"
| table _time, employee_id, psp_name, reader_id, in_window
```

Understanding this SPL

**Physical Access Outside Approved Maintenance Window at PSP (CIP-006-6 R1 Part 1.2)** — We alert on badge-granted entries at Physical Security Perimeters outside documented maintenance windows for follow-up with operations.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=physical` `sourcetype=pacs:access`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: physical; **sourcetype**: pacs:access. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=physical, sourcetype=pacs:access, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hour** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **dow** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where in_window!="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: _time, dow, employee_id, hour, in_window, psp_name, reader_id.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (after-hours), Table (events), Single value (count)
